Patch Set 10:

I used a macro earlier (see patch 8 and James feedback). So the stats in each fucntion are different. Ok now I am thinking, yes an inline function called from each of these show function should be better.

Patch Set 10:

sysfs kobjects are being created after the NIs/peers are configured successfully. i think it is better this way since the sysfs structure would show only whatever exists at a time and we will not have to deal with the removal of the kobjects if an NI/peer does not get configured because of some issue.

Patch Set 9:

One of the concerns I have is that we're going to have one function per stat. This will result in an explosion of functions, when you take into considerations peer statistics, as well as the health statistics coming down. Is that the best way of doing it?

Patch Set 11:

Reducing the function count is less important than readability. While we will have more functions they will simple be a few lines each that can be reviewed very easily.

parents.subject: LU-9667 lnet: Error out on missing network device information

commit.subject: LU-9667 lnet: Add LNet net sysfs structure
commit.message:
LU-9667 lnet: Add LNet net sysfs structure

Adding APIs to create ksets, kobjects
and the attribute files for net sysfs directory
structure.

Callback functions for the LNet NI attributes
are added to be used to display statistics.

Change-Id: Id777dd799ab95d96f84d9513b7fb470bc5502ab6
Test-Parameters: forbuildonly
Signed-off-by: Sonia Sharma <sharmaso@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/31744
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Amir Shehata <ashehata@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
